rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current AADL scope before generating AGREE expressions.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning expression types with declared variable types in AGREE contracts.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; move valid assignments to implementation annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the input AADL model.
generic_rules: Validate that all AGREE expressions reference only visible AADL features or previously declared helpers.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE contracts: numeric variables must be assigned numeric expressions.
Place component type contracts in type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
Remove invalid or unsafe clauses when diagnostics indicate unresolved references or type errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only declared constants, variables, or visible features in AGREE expressions.
Helper functions and variables must be defined within the same annex or imported.
Assignments in implementation scope must target valid output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not generate AGREE expressions that reference non-existent AADL features or types.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes.
Do not assume numeric bounds or types unless explicitly stated in the requirement or AADL model.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque data ports into numeric comparisons without explicit scalar representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Model fusion and insertion location decisions
